behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_WOOL::look' (examine) event for the Wool item: when a player (or relevant actor) examines the wool, the script builds and emits a one-line description "kith silk ".
Before emitting the text it adjusts per-call message/context flags (info, process-exclude, pid) so the bark is delivered with the intended routing/visibility semantics.

The description string is allocated/constructed, passed to the item-level bark routine (Item::bark) together with the item context, then freed. The handler then yields/suspends as a typical event process.
notable_control_flow: Entry is event-driven: triggered by the 'U8P_WOOL::look' event for the Wool item (no looping or long-running behavior).
Synchronous sequence: set up message/context flags -> construct string -> call Item::bark -> free string -> process return value (small implies/pop handling) -> suspend/return.
No iteration, no global state mutation observed; the routine confines effects to emitting a localized description and manipulating ephemeral call-context flags.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the 'info' and 'process-exclude' context flags: what recipients are excluded or what metadata 0x210 (if present) denotes in engine terms?
Whether the string is allocated via a general allocator that must be freed with a particular engine API (the disassembly implies freeing but exact free function name is not present).
Whether Item::bark does any additional side-effects beyond presenting the string (e.g., logging, AI notifications) — inferred as a display/emit function but full effects are external.

main_function.name: U8P_WOOL::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_WOOL::look' (examine) event for a Wool item by producing a short descriptive bark ('kith silk '), using the item context as the source and adjusting message/context flags to control delivery.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the object's 'U8P_WOOL::look' trigger (fired when the Avatar or relevant actor examines the wool)

function_map.name: U8P_WOOL::look
function_map.role: Event handler that constructs and emits the wool item's short description when examined.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to display/emit a short, single-line message from an item/process to a target (used here to present 'kith silk ').

external_calls.purpose: Deliver/display the constructed short description string ('kith silk ') from the Wool item to the viewing process/actor.
Set per-call message/context flags (info, process-exclude, pid) that control routing/visibility of the bark. This entry represents engine-level ops observed prior to calling Item::bark.
Free the temporary/constructed string after passing it to Item::bark (disassembly implies freeing but exact API name is not present).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_WOOL::look' (examine) event for the Wool item: when a player (or relevant actor) examines the wool, the script builds and emits a one-line description "kith silk ".
